guides.title: Understanding Dubai IBAN Structure: Format, Examples, and Validation
guides.short_description: Learn how Dubai IBANs are structured, how to read each part, and how to validate an IBAN before sending transfers.
guides.content:
## Overview
Dubai IBANs follow the standardized IBAN format defined for the United Arab Emirates (AE). This guide explains each segment of the IBAN and how it relates to local bank and branch codes.

## Dubai IBAN format
A typical UAE IBAN is 23 characters long and starts with the country code 'AE'. The general structure is:

`AEkk bbbb cccc cccc cccc ccc`

- `AE` – Country code for the United Arab Emirates
- `kk` – Two check digits
- `bbbb` – 4-digit bank identifier code
- `cccc cccc cccc ccc` – 17-digit domestic account number

## Example: Dubai account format
For a Dubai-based account, the IBAN still uses the UAE-wide structure. Bank and branch are encoded in the underlying account number, while the 4-digit bank code identifies the institution.

Example:

`AE07 0331 2345 6789 0123 456`

- Bank code: `0331`
- Account number: `234567890123456`

## Sample IBAN breakdown
In this section we break down the example IBAN field by field, showing how bank code, branch code, and account number are derived from a Dubai account.

1. Country code: `AE`
2. Check digits: `07`
3. Bank identifier: `0331` (e.g., Emirates NBD)
4. Basic bank account number (BBAN): `234567890123456`

We also show how online IBAN tools can reconstruct bank and branch information from the BBAN using Dubai bank code tables.

## Common mistakes
Common errors include transposed digits in the bank code, omitting leading zeros in the account number, and mis-typing the check digits. Using an IBAN validator before sending a transfer significantly reduces rejection risk.

## FAQ
**Q: Are Dubai IBANs different from Abu Dhabi IBANs?**
A: No. All UAE IBANs follow the same national format. The underlying account and branch data determine the emirate.

**Q: Do I still need the SWIFT code if I have the IBAN?**
A: For many cross-border transfers, both the IBAN and the SWIFT/BIC code of the receiving bank are required.

guides.title: SWIFT / BIC Codes for Dubai Banks: How to Find the Right One
guides.short_description: Understand SWIFT/BIC codes for Dubai banks, including how branch codes differ from general bank codes.
guides.content:
## What is a SWIFT / BIC code?
A SWIFT or BIC code is an 8- or 11-character identifier used to route international transfers.

## General vs. branch-specific codes
Many Dubai banks use a general SWIFT code (8 characters) plus an optional 3-character branch suffix for specific locations.

## How to look up a branch SWIFT code
Use the Bank & Branch Search or Branch Locator on this site to open the branch detail page and view the SWIFT section.

## Common SWIFT code patterns in Dubai
Most UAE banks use 'AE' as the country code within the BIC and 'AD' or 'AA' as the location code.
toc_sections: What is a SWIFT / BIC code?
How to look up a branch SWIFT code
Common SWIFT code patterns in Dubai

guides.title: Dubai Routing / Local Branch Codes: Where to Find Them
guides.short_description: Learn how Dubai banks use routing and local branch codes for domestic transfers and salary payments.
guides.content:
## Introduction
Routing or local branch codes are used for domestic transfers within the UAE, especially for salary and corporate payments.

## Format of local branch codes
Dubai branch codes are typically 3 to 5 digits. Some banks embed the branch code in the account number.

## Where routing codes appear
You can find routing codes on bank statements, salary letters, and branch detail pages on this site.

## Correcting routing code errors
If you notice an incorrect routing code, use the 'Report an issue with these details' link on the branch page.

guides.title: How to Compare Dubai Bank Transfer Fees by Branch
guides.short_description: Step-by-step instructions for comparing international transfer fees across Dubai branches.
guides.content:
## Why branch-level fees matter
Different branches of the same bank may apply different fee schedules depending on service type and customer segment.

## Using the Compare fees tool
The Compare fees page lets you filter by bank name, fee type, and maximum fee amount.

## Sorting by lowest transfer fee
After applying filters, sort branches by 'Transfer fee (low to high)' to quickly identify the cheapest option.

## Saving preferred branches
Use the 'Add to favorites' or 'Save branch' buttons to store your preferred low-fee branches.

guides.title: Using the Dubai Branch Locator for Area, Currency, and Hours
guides.short_description: Find branches by area such as Deira or Dubai Marina, filter by services, currencies, and opening hours.
guides.content:
## Accessing the Branch Locator
From the main navigation, open the Branch Locator to search by area, services, and hours.

## Filtering by area
Use the 'Area' dropdown to select locations like Deira, Dubai Marina, Jumeirah, and more.

## Filtering by services and currencies
Open the Services panel to filter for corporate or retail banking. Use the Currency filter for AED, USD, EUR, and other currencies.

## Viewing closing times and late-opening branches
Use the 'More filters' option to filter by closing time and find branches that stay open until or after 8:00 PM.
toc_sections: Viewing closing times and late-opening branches

guides.title: How to Validate a UAE IBAN and Find the Linked Branch
guides.short_description: Learn how to use the IBAN Validator tool to check an IBAN and navigate to the associated bank and branch.
guides.content:
## Opening the IBAN Tools
Navigate to the IBAN Tools section from the main navigation and choose 'IBAN Validator'.

## Entering an IBAN
Paste or type your IBAN in the input field and click 'Validate IBAN'.

## Reading validation results
The tool checks length, country format, and check digits, and shows the bank code and account number.

## Navigating to the bank
From the result, click the linked bank name to open the bank overview and view related branches.

guides.title: Bookmarking Guides and Saving Branches to Your List
guides.short_description: Keep track of important guides and preferred branches by using bookmarks and the Saved page.
guides.content:
## Bookmarking guides
Each guide includes a 'Bookmark this guide' button near the title. Use it to add the article to your reading list.

## Saving branches
On any branch detail page, use 'Add to favorites' or 'Save branch' to store it in your Saved view.

## Managing your saved items
Open the Saved page from the main navigation to review, remove, or organize saved branches and guides.

guides.title: Corporate Banking Branch Hours in Dubai: Deira, Marina, and More
guides.short_description: Overview of typical corporate banking hours in key Dubai areas, including late-opening branches.
guides.content:
## Standard corporate banking hours
Most corporate branches in Dubai operate from 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM, with extended hours in busy locations.

## Late-opening corporate branches
Some Dubai Marina and Downtown branches remain open until 8:00 PM or later.

## Using the closing time filter
The Branch Locator includes a 'Closing time' filter under 'More filters' to find late-opening options.

## Planning international transfers
Check both cut-off times and branch closing times when planning same-day international transfers.
